title: linum austriacum subsp. marschallianum
intro: Linum austriacum subsp. marschallianum, commonly known as Marschall's flax or Austrian flax, is a plant species within the genus Linum, which is a type of flowering plant in the family Linaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to its high content of mucilages and saponins. The health benefits of Linum austriacum subsp. marschallianum include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and promote wound healing. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or topical creams, and are often used to treat various skin conditions, digestive issues, and respiratory problems.
